title: Metabolomic Analysis and Compound Annotation Protocol
author: Alice M.S. RODRIGUES
materials: Fragment list (MS/MS spectra linked to parent ions)
Two complementary strategies were applied for compound annotation:
Spectral annotation tool within FBMN workflow.
Proposed formulas and structures are cross-checked against:
MS 1 and MS 2 spectra are analyzed in FreeStyle® (Thermo Fisher Scientific) for adduct identification and formula validation.
Neighboring ions in the network are examined.

steps.title: Software used
steps.instruction: Software used: MZmine 4 (v4.8.30) was used to preprocess UHPLC-MS/MS raw data.

steps.title: Platform
steps.instruction: Platform: Processed data from MZmine were uploaded to the GNPS platform (Global Natural Products Social Molecular Network, https://gnps.ucsd.edu).

steps.title: Feature-Based Molecular Networking (FBMN) can be...
steps.instruction: Feature-Based Molecular Networking (FBMN) can be used to construct molecular networks, treating each feature independently (Nothias et al., 2020).

steps.title: Network visualization
steps.instruction: Network visualization: Molecular networks are visualized using Cytoscape (open-source software from the Institute for Systems Biology, https://cytoscape.org).

steps.title: Matches experimental MS/MS spectra with theoretical...
steps.instruction: Matches experimental MS/MS spectra with theoretical spectra generated in silico from known natural product structures.

steps.title: Annotates a compound when a match...
steps.instruction: Annotates a compound when a match is found (Allard et al., 2016; Mohimani et al., 2017).

steps.title: Predicts molecular formulas, structural candidates, or...
steps.instruction: Predicts molecular formulas, structural candidates, or chemical classes based on MS/MS fragmentation data.

steps.title: Allows insertion of individual MS/MS spectra...
steps.instruction: Allows insertion of individual MS/MS spectra and matches against public experimental databases.

steps.title: Fragmentation patterns of each ion are manually interpreted to:
steps.instruction: Fragmentation patterns of each ion are manually interpreted to:

steps.title: Based on structural similarity and fragmentation...
steps.instruction: Based on structural similarity and fragmentation logic, molecular modifications are inferred and propagated to propose annotations for related compounds.
